rulings.Case Title: PETER ZOLA VS. JOHN BRENT ELLIOT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Peter Zola'S Motion To Overrule Objections To Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records To: 1) Old Republic Title Company; 2) First Republic Bank
rulings.Rulings: December 16, 2016 Line 7: Plaintiff Peter Zola'S Motion To Overrule Objections To Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records To: 1) Old Republic Title Company; 2) First Republic Bank Pro Tem Judge Aaron Minnis, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. Lauren M. Elliott's objections to the subpoenas duces tecum issued to Old Republic Title Company and First Republic Bank are overruled. The records requested shall be handled confidentially to address Ms. Elliott's privacy concerns. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to aaron@minnisandsmallets.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (jpt/302).

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTOPHER J.S EMBY VS. FRANCESCA M MARSH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion In Support Of Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Plaintiffs Request For Admissions, Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Francesca M. Marshs Responses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories, Set No 1, First Set Of Specially Prepared Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents, Compelling Defendant To Produce Documents For Inspection, Copying, Testing Or Sampling, And Imposing Sanctions On The Defendant And Her Attorney
rulings.Rulings: December 16, 2019 Line 3: PLAINTIFF CHRISTOPHER EMBY'S Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Plaintiffs Request For Admissions, Motion For Order Compelling Defendant Francesca M. Marshs Responses To Plaintiffs Form Interrogatories, Set No 1, First Set Of Specially Prepared Interrogatories And Request For Production Of Documents, Compelling Defendant To Produce Documents For Inspection, Copying, Testing Or Sampling, And Imposing Sanctions On The Defendant And Her Attorney Pro Tem Judge Aaron Minnis, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted, no opposition filed. Request for Admissions Nos. 1-44 are deemed admitted as to Defendant Francesca Marsh. Defendant shall provide verified, Code-compliant responses to form interrogatories (set one), special interrogatories (set one), and requests for production of documents (set one), without objections except privilege, and responsive documents within 30 days. Defendant Marsh shall pay sanctions to Michael B. Bassi, A Law Corporation, in the amount of $1,500 within 30 days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to aaron@minnisandsmallets.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (jpt/302).

rulings.Case Title: LOUISE WILLIAMS VS. NIBBI BROTHERS CONSTRUCTION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Number One And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: December 16, 2019 Line 4: PLAINTIFF LOUISE WILLIAMS' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Number One And Request For Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Aaron Minnis, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. The motion is timely because Defendant's responses state that Defendant will produce responsive, non-privileged documents in its possession or control. See Standon Co. v. Superior Court, 225 Cal. App. 3d 898, 903 (holding that no time limit is placed on a motion to compel compliance.) Defendant shall produce responsive, non-privileged documents within 10 days. The photos of Plaintiff, the photos of the scene, and the incident report by Ms. Hilaski are not privileged because they do not derive from an attorney's work for Defendant or reflect the attorney's evaluation or interpretation of the law or facts. See Nacht & Lewis Architects, Inc. v. Superior Court, 47 Cal. App. 4th 214, 218 (holding that "statements written or recorded independently by witnesses neither reflect an attorney's evaluation of the case nor constitute derivative material, and therefore are neither absolute nor qualified work product.") Defendant shall provide a privilege log for any information being withheld on privilege grounds, within 10 days. The request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to aaron@minnisandsmallets.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (jpt/302).

rulings.Case Title: QORUM, INC. VS. QAPROSOFT, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, December 16, 2019, Line 11, CROSS DEFENDANTS QORUM, INC., ANDREW PIETRA'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Cross-defendants Qorum Inc. and Andrew Pietra's demurrer to all eight casues of action in the cross-complaint filed by QaProsoft, LLC and Solvd, Inc. is sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the first cause of action for breach of contract alleged by Solvd, sustained without leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action for violation of Penal Code 496, and overruled as to all other causes of action. The breach of contract claim alleged by Solvd, a non-party to the contract, lacks ultimate facts to support Solvd's ability to sue as a successor. There is no allegation why Solvd is a successor to QaProsoft. The second through fourth causes of action for common counts are adequately plead. Cross-Complainants are entitled to plead facts or legal theories in the alternative, or even inconsistently. (Radar Co. v. Stone (1986) 178 Cal.App.3d 10, 29.) The fifth cause of action for violation of the Invasion of Privacy Act is also sufficient. (Kight v. CashCall, Inc. (2011) 200 Cal.App.4th 1377, 1391 ["[T]he statute prevents a corporation and an individual from secretly monitoring a conversation, regardless whether the individual is acting for his or her own purposes or on behalf of the corporation."]) The Penal Code cause of action is barred by Lacagnina v. Comprehend Systems, Inc. (2018) 25 Cal.App.5th 955. The seventh and eighth causes of action for false promise and violation of the Unfair Competition Law are adequately alleged. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the cross-complainants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: THIRD STREET DATA, INC. D/B/A TOPFUNNEL VS. GEORGE CAROLLO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Motion To Compel More Particularized Identification Of Trade Secrets Pursuant To C.C.P. 2019.210, Enforce Protective Order, And Stay Discovery *** Redacted Version ***
rulings.Rulings: December 16, 2019 Line 6: DEFENDANTS GEORGE CAROLLO, STAYA, INC. D/B/A DOVER'S Motion To Compel More Particularized Identification Of Trade Secrets Pursuant To C.C.P. 2019.210, Enforce Protective Order, And Stay Discovery Pro Tem Judge Aaron Minnis, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Granted. Plaintiff's trade secrets designation is not reasonably particular. Plaintiff shall furnish a revised designation within 30 days. The revised designation should include a summary in plain language of the specific trade secrets at issue and a numbered list of the trade secrets with a corresponding list of specific elements for each. The revised designation need not explain how the alleged trade secrets differ from general knowledge in the trade but must include enough detail to enable Defendants to investigate and make that determination. Defendants' request to modify the protective order is denied, without prejudice to Defendant to challenge any confidentiality designations that Plaintiff may make to the revised designation. Discovery by Plaintiff is stayed pending service of the revised designation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to aaron@minnisandsmallets.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (jpt/302).
